diagrams.title: 2-to-4 Decoder
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a 2-to-4 decoder circuit showing the two input lines (A, B), enable input (E), and four output lines (Y0, Y1, Y2, Y3). The diagram illustrates how each unique combination of inputs activates exactly one output, demonstrating the decoder's one-hot output property.
elements: Output lines Y0, Y1, Y2, Y3
Logic gate symbols (AND gates with inverters)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental operation of a decoder - how n input lines can select one of 2^n output lines, which is essential for address decoding and demultiplexing in computer systems.

diagrams.title: 4-to-2 Encoder
diagrams.description: A diagram showing a 4-to-2 priority encoder with four input lines (D0, D1, D2, D3) and two output lines (A, B). The illustration includes the priority logic, showing how the encoder converts multiple input signals into a binary code, with D3 having highest priority.
elements: Input lines D0, D1, D2, D3
Truth table showing all input-output combinations
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how an encoder performs the inverse operation of a decoder, converting multiple input lines into a compact binary representation, which is crucial for interrupt handling and keyboard encoding.

diagrams.title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer
diagrams.description: A visual diagram of a 4-to-1 multiplexer (MUX) displaying four data input lines (D0, D1, D2, D3), two select lines (S0, S1), and one output (Y). The diagram shows the selection logic and how the select lines determine which data input is routed to the output.
elements: Data input lines D0, D1, D2, D3
diagrams.purpose: Explains how a multiplexer selects one of multiple data sources based on select lines, demonstrating data routing concepts essential for building data paths and implementing conditional logic in processors.
